workflows.name: Project Work
workflows.description: Inspect, fix, or test a configured local ALM checkout.
workflows.context: Local project workflow. Work only in the selected local project path unless the task explicitly widens scope.

operations.name: Inspect
operations.description: Inspect the selected project and report findings.

operations.name: Fix
operations.description: Make a scoped fix in the selected project and run relevant checks.

operations.name: Test
operations.description: Run requested tests or checks in the selected project.

operations.name: Custom Project Task
operations.description: Run a custom task in the selected project.

workflows.name: VB
workflows.description: Analyze and prepare VB/VBCS ALM Git bug fixes from natural-language instructions.
workflows.context: Plain-English VB/VBCS bug workflow for ALM Git projects. Treat the Task text as authoritative natural-language instructions and infer the bug number, base branch, important files, regression flag, requested checks, and desired output from the wording. First runs are read-only: use BugDB MCP for bug details, verify the selected local ALM Git checkout and requested base branch, inspect important files against origin/<base_branch>, and propose the exact branch, worktree, fix, test, push, and ALM MR sequence with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input before changing state. For ALM Git projects, GitHub is only the trigger/control plane; the source repository is the selected local ALM checkout. Verify origin points to ALM, refresh ALM refs with git fetch origin <base_branch> or git fetch origin as needed, create branches/worktrees from the refreshed origin/<base_branch>, and do not run generic git pull by default. Use standard ALM transaction branch names for pushed review branches and ALM MR review_branch values: Integration-<release> becomes ${USER}_int<release>-<bug_number>, maintenance-<release> becomes ${USER}_mnt<release>-<bug_number>, and main becomes ${USER}_main-<bug_number>. After approval, keep the selected project checkout clean and create one per-bug branch plus one per-bug worktree under /scratch/<user>/codex-github-trigger/worktrees/<project>/<bug>/ using that standard branch name. Stop if the branch, remote branch, or worktree already exists unless the user approves reuse. For regression issues, inspect the last 30 first-parent merge commits on refreshed ALM origin/<base_branch>, parse Merge-Request subjects, and rank likely culprit MRs by touched files and BugDB keywords. Use vblens-engineer and VBLens MCP for VBCS/VB code analysis, HTML/JavaScript review, MR issue retrieval, and component-aware guidance. For employment-* UI projects, use VBLens family=HCM and product=ALL. Read VB default reviewers from /scratch/<USER>/codeshare/vb_defaultreviewers.txt, then create ALM MRs with the local-runner-owned scripts/alm-scripts/create_alm_mr.py helper after the approved fix branch is pushed; if the file is missing or empty, stop with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input and ask the requester to populate VB default reviewers. The helper canonicalizes reviewers to lowercase Oracle emails, checks for an existing MR, verifies the branch is ahead, omits relatedIssues by default to avoid ALM HTTP 500, and falls back to status New with short_description exactly Merge Request for '<review_branch>'. Use ALM MCP prepare_create_merge_request/confirm_create_merge_request only if the helper is unavailable, with the same lowercase reviewers from /scratch/<USER>/codeshare/vb_defaultreviewers.txt and no related_issues. Do not use git push -o or git push --push-option because this runner's Git 2.9.2 does not support push options.

workflows.name: Backport
workflows.description: Backport one base BugDB bug to a numeric maintenance release using VB or ADE.
workflows.context: Approval-gated Backport workflow. Backport type is mandatory: VB follows the existing ALM Git/VB maintenance backport path, while ADE follows the ADE branch backport path. Base bug number is mandatory. Target release is composed from Release year LOV 26, 27, 28, 29, 30 and Release month LOV 01, 04, 07, 10, then emitted as yy.mm such as 26.07; compact values such as 2607 may be accepted only for compatibility. For VB, first run is read-only: read the base BugDB bug, locate exactly one ALM transaction/MR or ask for the MR URL, verify the selected local ALM checkout origin points to ALM, refresh ALM refs with git fetch origin maintenance-<release> or git fetch origin as needed, verify origin/maintenance-<release>, verify branch/worktree availability, and propose the numbered command sequence with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input. Do not switch to a GitHub remote or run generic git pull by default for ALM Git backports; create the worktree branch from refreshed origin/maintenance-<release>. After approval, create the backport bug with base_rptno=<base bug>, add REL13PREARU-<release> and FWD tags, cherry-pick into an isolated worktree branch, push to the ALM origin remote, and create an ALM MR with the local-runner-owned scripts/alm-scripts/create_alm_mr.py helper using reviewers read from /scratch/<USER>/codeshare/vb_defaultreviewers.txt; if the file is missing or empty, stop with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input and ask the requester to populate VB default reviewers. For ADE, first read-only preflight checks /ade_autofs/ud522_fa/FUSIONAPPS_HWM<YYMM>_LINUX.X64.rdd first, then /ade_autofs/ud522_fa/FUSIONAPPS_PT.V2MIBHCMPREARU13<YYMM>_LINUX.X64.rdd; prefer HWM when present and stop with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input if neither exists. HWM ADE path creates a view on the HWM latest label, creates a new BugDB bug with base_rptno, runs ade begintrans -backport <new_bug_number> -fromtrans <user>_bug-<base_bug> -bl <view_label> -nocheckout, sets README_BUGFIX_FUNCT_DESC from a summarized base-bug functional summary file, sets BugDB status 51, Gen/Prt I, Tracking backport, fills BugSmart closure, and runs bug validation. PREARU ADE path creates a PREARU view and transaction for the new bug, creates a new BugDB bug with base_rptno, sets Tracking type FWD, sets README_BUGFIX_FUNCT_DESC, and fills BugSmart closure. Do not expose HWM versus PREARU as a UI choice.

operations.name: Backport
operations.description: Prepare an approval-gated backport for the base BugDB bug and target release.

workflows.name: SeedData
workflows.description: Create approval-gated Profile Option, Message, Lookup, or Value Set seed data changes.
workflows.context: Approval-gated SeedData workflow. Seed Data Type is mandatory and controls the requested fields: Profile Option, Message, Lookup, or Value Set. Profile Option, Lookup, and Value Set support Copy from staging Yes/No. If Copy from staging is No, Profile Option uses generated profile SQL, Message uses requester-supplied SQL, Lookup uses generated lookup SQL from row-based lookup values with optional display sequence, and Value Set stops after load/prep for manual value-set creation. If Copy from staging is Yes, the Staging environment name resolves through seeddata_staging_environments.json in the sibling codex_local_runner repo; default environment is Bronze Manual, Custom uses the task-supplied Staging URL, default username is fusion_read_only, default password is Welcome1!, and the runner should create or reuse DB link CODEX_SEEDDATA_STAGING in the local fusion DB after load/prep and before extract using the resolved staging URL. Dynamic staging key fields are Profile option code / PROFILE_OPTION_NAME, Lookup type / LOOKUP_TYPE, and Value set code / VALUE_SET_CODE. Staging copy table sets are Profile Option: FND_PROFILE_OPTIONS_B, FND_PROFILE_OPTIONS_TL, FND_PROFILE_OPTION_VALUES, FND_PROFILE_OPTION_LEVELS; Lookup: FND_LOOKUP_TYPES, FND_LOOKUP_TYPES_TL, FND_LOOKUP_VALUES_B, FND_LOOKUP_VALUES_TL; Value Set: FND_VS_VALUE_SETS, FND_VS_VT_TABLE_EXT, FND_VS_VT_TABLE_BO_EXT, FND_VS_VT_VO_EXT, FND_VS_VALUES_B, FND_VS_VALUES_TL, FND_VS_RELATED_SETS, FND_VS_RELATED_VALUES. Use local sequence remaps FND_PROFILE_OPTIONS_S, FND_VS_VALUE_SETS_S, and FND_VS_VALUES_B_S where local surrogate keys are needed. Product is fixed as PER; default LBA is HcmEmploymentTop. Use sibling codex_local_runner/seeddata_workflow.py to generate SQL, script seed types, exact load/extract script commands, staging copy guidance, and bug-number ADE transaction names; use loadSeedData.sh and extractSeedData.sh instead of hand-building Java commands. For any post-view SeedData command through RunInView.sh, use helper-generated view commands with timeout 3600 and --seeddata-env. First run is read-only: validate /scratch/<user>/codeshare/dbdetails.txt, /scratch/<user>/codeshare/ade_defaultreviewers.txt, and /scratch/<user>/codeshare/SeedDataComparator.class, parse DB host/port/service, resolve target ADE label series, derive partitionkey_value from the seed XML when needed, create a BugDB bug only when an existing bug number was not provided, use <username>_bug-<bug_number> as the ADE transaction name, search ADE for a unique existing view with that transaction and reuse it when found, and propose the exact BugDB, ADE, SQL/staging/manual creation, load/extract scripts, comparator, report upload, checkin, and OraReview sequence with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input. If multiple ADE views match the transaction, stop with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input and ask for the view choice. Before running Lookup SQL or lookup staging copy, verify FND_APPLICATION rows for AU and PER and use local AU application_id for lookup verification and metadata updates. After extract, run SeedDataComparator from a writable report directory with java -cp /scratch/<user>/codeshare/projects/codex_local_runner SeedDataComparator --ref=<target-dir>/.ade_path/<seed-file.xml> --mod=<extracted-seed-file.xml> --exclude=StartDate,ProgramName, inspect <SeedFile>_Reports.zip, derive the paired XLF as US/<SeedFile>.xlf, checkout and copy both the XML and XLF when the extracted XLF exists, and upload it to BugDB. If the extracted XLF exists but the ADE target XLF is missing, stop with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input before creating a new XLF element. Do not echo staging passwords in comments or logs. Stop on any BugDB, ADE, SQL, staging copy, comparator, upload, checkin, or OraReview failure.

operations.name: Seed Data
operations.description: Prepare an approval-gated seed data change for Profile Option, Message, Lookup, or Value Set seed data.

workflows.name: Custom Commands
workflows.description: Run configured command intents or request approval for a natural-language command plan.
workflows.context: Custom command workflow. Use the selected run context and the validated command catalog. The runner starts GitHub Codex sessions with Native Codex workspace-write access plus configured ADE/FMW writable roots; the approval boundary is the issue workflow, command catalog, and follow-up approval comments. Catalog commands are approved to run in order. Direct natural-language catalog intents are approved when exactly one intent matches and required parameters are unambiguous. Skill upgrades are handled by the GitHub runner at process startup/restart; do not run local skill update commands inside a GitHub-triggered Codex session. For natural-language custom tasks, infer the relevant skill or MCP from the request text, project context, follow-up comments, and available ADE or transaction details. Use read-only MCP tools directly for information gathering, status, report-card, review-comment, and code-search questions. For new BugDB bug creation, use the local-runner-owned bugdb_create_bug.py helper. Subject-only creation is valid through bugdb_create_bug.py --description '<subject>' with the local-runner-owned bugdb_create_payload.json defaults, so do not ask for a complete create payload unless the default payload is missing, the requester asks to override fields outside the default, or the request is ambiguous. Use BugDB MCP first for existing bug reads, summaries, comments, and field updates when the needed MCP tool exists. If the MCP session cannot update existing bug fields, an approved BugDB field update may use the dev-bugdb-helper skill CLI at $CODEX_HOME/skills/dev-bugdb-helper/scripts/run-bugdb.sh; CODEX_HOME should be the scratch path /scratch/<runner-user>/.codex. Use /bin/python3.11 only through that skill wrapper, do not change global python3, do not print OAuth tokens or client secrets, read the current bug/header fields before mutation, and resolve friendly field labels such as Bug Fix ETA, Bug Fix By, or Component Version to exact BugDB API keys before constructing the update payload. For product_id 2419, use cached metadata at docs/bugdb_header_fields_2419.json; call header-fields only if the cache is missing or does not contain the requested label. Do not guess field keys. Update only the requested fields while preserving required BugDB fields, then verify with a readback and rerun any gated validation. For first OraReview creation, no existing review is expected; if orareview -x or orareview -t <txn> -x says no review exists after the ADE view, transaction, saved changes, and reviewers are verified, read default ADE/OraReview reviewers from /scratch/<USER>/codeshare/ade_defaultreviewers.txt when the requester did not supply an explicit reviewer list, continue with CreateOra.sh, and verify status after creation. For OraReview merge readiness, one current reviewer approval is sufficient unless OraReview or the merge tooling reports an explicit blocking status such as a rejection, unresolved mandatory-review requirement, stale approval, wrong transaction, or open review issue; do not stop only because another non-mandatory reviewer has not approved. If CodeSearch is unavailable or inconclusive for ADE file discovery, use the user's supplied path or intent and read-only inspection under /ade_autofs/ud522_fa/FUSIONAPPS_PT.V2MIBHCMBRONZE_LINUX.X64.rdd/LATEST/ before the ADE view exists. Use the local-runner-owned scripts/ade-scripts/RunInView.sh wrapper instead of the deployed ADE Lens RunInView.sh. The local wrapper enters the ADE view with an EOF/heredoc-style ade useview session, starts from the view's fusionapps root, can block while ADE prepares the view after cleanview, and should batch sequential ADE commands when there is no cleanview or approval boundary; required output checkpoints should be enforced inline inside the same loaded ADE session where practical. For regular ADE commands through this wrapper, it defaults FUSIONAPPS_SKIP_FMWC=TRUE to avoid full FMWC prep wait time; actual PMC setup/run commands should pass --full-fmwc. When preparing a command for this wrapper, if a repository-relative path starts with fusionapps/, pass it without the leading fusionapps/. For ADE view locks such as ade_info.vmd.free or Locked by process id, suspect ade persist first: run ade persist -list outside any view, close the matching full view with ade persist -c <viewname>, then retry once. Do not remove ade_locks.wrd automatically. After ADE code edits, inspect the transaction delta with ade lsco or ade describetrans -diff_txn_changes <txn_name>. If any changed file is a Java source file (.java), run the smallest available Java compile check inside the same confirmed ADE view before finalizing. Prefer the owning module or product build command that supplies the Fusion classpath; for HCM, use the documented HCM build or manual build path when the active view has $ADE_VIEW_ROOT/fusionapps/hcm/build-core.xml and $ADE_VIEW_ROOT/fusionapps/hcm/build-coresetup.xml. Do not count standalone javac without the Fusion classpath as proof. If the basic compile cannot run, fails, or times out, report the command, reason, and remaining risk. HCM bug validation blocks later work unless it exits 0 and outputs standalone PASS; non-PASS results require follow-up verification, BugDB updates through MCP when available or dev-bugdb-helper when MCP lacks field-update support, and validation rerun. HCM PMC must first identify the unique ADE view and transaction named rnerolu_bug-<bugid>, summarize the exact PMC command sequence, and ask for confirmation before running it. For natural-language state-changing requests without catalog commands, propose a numbered command sequence first and ask for approval with CODEX_TRIGGER_STATUS: needs-input. After approval arrives in a follow-up comment and the issue is tagged followup-codex, continue in the same session and run the approved sequence.
